title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at the House of Blues with openers The Chats on March 20, 2026.
The concert featured fan favorites and new songs, with enthusiastic responses from the audience.
Howlin' Pelle Almqvist led the show, emphasizing the band's commitment to entertaining and engaging fans.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, from Braintree, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
This will be McHugh’s third Boston Marathon after overcoming Stage 3 breast cancer diagnosed in 2023.
McHugh previously ran for the Joe Andruzzi Foundation, inspired by her late uncle's cancer battle.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey urges Meta to address privacy concerns with facial recognition in smart glasses.
Markey, Wyden, and Merkley worry technology may identify protesters, discourage public dissent.
Senators demand Meta disclose data handling practices by April 6; facial recognition plans remain unclear.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy city council delayed a $2.65 million vote for new firefighting gear after current equipment tested positive for PFAS.
Mayor Thomas Koch proposed replacing gear to prevent health risks, but councilors voted 5-4 to keep the plan in committee.
Firefighters and union leaders expressed frustration, citing continued exposure risks and urging protective measures for personnel.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Mayor Cathleen DeSimone proposes transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to address $57 million in necessary repairs.
The zoo, opened in 1937, houses over 100 animals, 70 of which are geriatric, with declining attendance impacting finances.
Zoo vet Bekah Weatherington criticizes the plan as unthought-out; a transition team is expected to form by July.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin Wong and Abigail Brown held a last-minute microwedding in Somerville due to a blizzard disrupting their original plans.
FX series 'Love Story' explores John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette's relationship, reflecting on 1990s communication methods.
Discussion in Love Letters delves into the appropriateness of using 'partner' vs. 'boyfriend' in modern relationships.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, 44, from Burlington, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH to honor his father.
After his father's death to glioblastoma, Averback survived testicular cancer, reshaping his views on time and health.
Despite a serious blood infection, Averback resumed training, emphasizing the importance of forward motion in his journey.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Patriots are reportedly interested in trading for Eagles WR A.J. Brown, a three-time Pro Bowler.
New Patriots safety Kevin Byard has been working out with A.J. Brown, fueling trade speculation.
Philadelphia may wait until June to trade Brown to manage salary cap, seeking a first-round pick.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens completed his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
The Boston Bruins may sign Hagens, the seventh pick in the 2025 NHL Draft, soon.
Hagens could provide a boost to the Bruins as they approach their final regular-season matches.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' starring Cillian Murphy is now streaming on Netflix.
'Is This Thing On?' featuring Will Arnett and directed by Bradley Cooper is streaming on Disney+.
'Born to Bowl,' a new bowling docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber, is available on HBO Max.
